description: Interactive personality-based or choice-driven polls that invite audience to self-identify with product attributes or make preference selections, driving direct comment engagement.
structural_recipe: Create a binary or multiple-choice question that asks followers to identify which product variant matches their personality or preference. Frame using 'أيهم' (which one) or 'من ومين تنصحون' (who do you recommend) patterns. Include personality descriptors or celebratory occasions. Use emojis relevant to product category. Include branded hashtags with vernacular Arabic (#مننا_ويفهم_جونا). Direct the question toward the audience to trigger response.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond strongly to self-referential and identity-based content. Personality matching creates emotional investment ('يشبهك'). Framing as recommendation-seeking leverages trust culture and community opinion-valuing. Holiday/occasion timing (Eid reference) increases cultural relevance. The interactive format bypasses passive scrolling and creates FOMO among non-commenters.
transplantation_caveats: Personality matching must align with Saudi cultural values—avoid attributes that conflict with religious or social norms
Holiday-pegged content requires accurate timing to Saudi Islamic calendar events
Self-identification language ('أيهم يشبهك') requires authentic Arabic phrasing; literal translations fail
Recommendation-seeking format assumes audience trust; brand must have established credibility first
cultural_constraints: Content must not position products in conflict with Islamic values
Personality descriptors should reflect positive Saudi social identity markers
Avoid gendered language unless intentionally segmented
